data.title: Schott Pharma climbs after Berenberg upgrades to 'buy'
data.description:
** Shares in Schott Pharma are up around 2.5% after Berenberg raised its rating on German pharmaceutical industry supplier to "buy" from "hold"

** The broker says the company is set to benefit from recovering drug containment demand

** Superiority of GLP-1 injectable diabetes drugs, structural trends in biologics and ready-to-use vials underpin sustainable long-term market growth, with peers projecting restocking and higher production volumes into 2026, it notes

** After having peaked in 2023, the broker expects Schott Pharma's capital expenditures to normalise at around 12-13% of sales in 2026-2027, positioning the company for stable free cash flow generation from 2026 onwards

** The broker adds it sees the company's valuation as "attractive"

** Out of 13 analysts that cover Schott Pharma, 11 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy," ​one rates it "hold" and one "sell" - LSEG data

data.title: Kia America to recall over 250,000 US vehicles over fuel tank leak
data.description:
Kia America is recalling 250,547 K5 vehicles in the U.S. over a damaged fuel tank posing a fire risk, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Thursday.

Affected fuel tanks will be inspected for damage and replaced, with dealers to replace the check valve, NHTSA said.

data.title: PZU rises as Q3 profit beat fuels dividend expectations
data.description:
** Polish state-run insurer PZU shares rise around 4%, on track for its best day since April, after it posted a third quarter profit beat, driven by lower-than-expected claims and benefits

** "Thanks to lower claims and benefits in the quarter, the company recorded very strong income from insurance services," Erste Group analyst Lukasz Janczak says

** Janczak adds that while the low loss ratio is not sustainable, the results should allow for an upgrade to full-year forecasts

** The analyst also says that the strong result confirms the potential for a high dividend payment next year

data.title: Nvidia's Impressive Performance Stretches Gap Against Peers — Market Talk
data.description: Nvidia delivered an impressive performance, suggesting that the performance gap versus peers is likely to widen, Deutsche Bank analyst Ross Seymore writes in a note. The chip maker's third-quarter beat to consensus and continued leadership in AI compute, networking, software, and systems remain notable strengths, he says. Management expressed strong confidence in future data-center growth, pointing to roughly $500 billion in cumulative revenue from early 2025 through the end of 2026, he notes. Deutsche Bank raises its 2026-27 revenue and earnings per share estimates by low-double-digit percentages. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Gulf stocks follow global rally; eyes on US job data
data.description:
By Md Manzer Hussain

Most Gulf equities rose in early trade on Thursday, tracking global peers as investors took heart from Nvidia's blockbuster earnings and looked ahead to delayed U.S. jobs data.

Overnight, Nvidia forecast quarterly revenue well above Wall Street expectations, with CEO Jensen Huang citing surging demand for its artificial intelligence chips from major cloud providers and downplaying fears of an AI bubble.

The Abu Dhabi benchmark index added 0.2%, lifted by a 14.8% jump in Eshraq Investments and a 5.8% gain in Presight AI Holding .

Sentiment towards the region's tech and AI sector was also underpinned after the U.S. Commerce Department said on Wednesday it had authorized the export of advanced AI chips to two companies in the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.

Dubai's benchmark stock index climbed 0.6%, with most constituents in positive territory. Emaar Properties rose 1.5%, while business parks operator TECOM Group advanced 1.6%.

Saudi Arabia's benchmark stock index edged 0.2% higher, with most sectors gaining. Riyadh Cables added 1.6% and Saudi Arabian Mining Co (Maaden) rose 1.9%.

Separately, MP Materials said it would build a rare earths refinery in Saudi Arabia in partnership with the U.S. Department of Defense and Maaden, aiming to expand Middle Eastern processing capacity for critical minerals.

The Qatari benchmark index fell 1%, dragged lower by broad-based declines. Industries Qatar lost 1.3%, while Qatar National Bank also slipped 1.3%.

Investors are awaiting the release of September's delayed U.S. non-farm payrolls report later in the day for clues on the Federal Reserve's next policy move. Fed funds futures now imply about a 33% chance of a 25-basis-point rate cut at the December 10 meeting, down from around 50% a day earlier.

Monetary policy in the United States is closely watched in the Gulf, where most currencies are pegged to the U.S. dollar.

data.title: Vietnam coffee harvest stalls as floods hit Central Highlands
data.description:
By Phuong Nguyen and Mas Alina Arifin

Fresh coffee supply from Vietnam’s harvest has yet to pick up as heavy rains in the Central Highlands slow progress, traders said on Thursday, while Indonesian farmers anticipate a mini harvest in four months.

A new round of floods triggered by torrential rain in central and Central Highlands Vietnam caused some coffee farms in the low-lying areas of Dak Lak to become flooded, which slowed down the harvest and prevented farmers from drying beans, Reuters reported.

“It’s been raining heavily here. Flood waters could wash away beans that fell during the downpour, causing huge losses for farmers,” said a trader in the coffee belt.

Vietnamese farmers sold beans at 112,500 dong to 113,500 dong ($4.27 to $4.30) per kg, compared with last week's 111,500 dong to 113,500 dong range.

"Farmers are unable to pick beans at the moment due to the rain," another trader based in the area said, adding that the rain is expected to affect the quality of the beans.

Robusta coffee for January delivery settled down $57, or 1%, at $4,516 a metric ton on Wednesday,

In Indonesia, Sumatra robusta coffee beans were offered at a $70 discount to the December contract, compared with a $55 discount last week as an adjusting to the London terminal, a trader said.

Another trader said that for the January 2026 contract, the beans were offered at a $50 discount, widening from $30 discount rate last week for the same period.

Coffee farmers from West Lampung said the weather was favourable for trees to grow and expected a mini harvest in four months.

($1 = 26,374.0000 dong)

data.title: Singapore fuel oil stockpiles hold well above 24 million barrels
data.description:
Onshore fuel oil stockpiles in major oil storage hub Singapore posted a weekly decline, but remained well above 24 million barrels, data showed on Thursday.

Inventories for residual fuels (STKRS-SIN) eased 5.5% to 24.52 million barrels (about 3.86 million metric tons) in the week to November 19, logging a two-week low, based on data from Enterprise Singapore.

The inventories continued to hover above a year-to-date average of about 22.5 million barrels per week.

Spot differentials for fuel oil were capped by high inventories. Differentials for high-sulphur fuel held steadily in steep discounts, while low-sulphur fuel oil differntials were rangebound this week.

Supplies from various regions to Asia remained plentiful. Total imports into onshore storage rose 47% week-on-week to more than 888,000 tons, with most inflows hailing from the Netherlands, Saudi Arabia and Indonesia.

Meanwhile, fuel oil exports from the tanks fell 29% to about 201,000 tons, with Bangladesh, China and Hong Kong as the key destinations.

data.title: Iron ore retreats from two-week high with supply glut concerns in focus
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices snapped a three-session winning streak on Thursday, with focus back to concerns over a supply glut in the fourth quarter amid robust shipment and weakening demand in top consumer China.

The most-traded January iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade 0.32% lower at 788.5 yuan ($110.83) a metric ton, after hitting a more than two-week high on Wednesday.

The benchmark December iron ore (SZZFZ5) on the Singapore Exchange was down 0.38% at $103.95 a ton, as of 0742 GMT.

Shipments of the key steelmaking ingredient to China have been robust with November imports expected to stay above 100 million tons for a sixth straight month, keeping China's total imports for the year on track for a new record.

Also, the massive Simandou iron ore project in Guinea began production last week, beefing up the anticipation of growing supply in the coming years.

By contrast, hot metal output, a gauge of iron ore demand, will likely fall in coming weeks, piling pressure on prices of raw materials, analysts at broker Galaxy Futures said in a note.

Additionally, China left benchmark lending rates unchanged on Thursday for the sixth consecutive month in November, meeting market expectations.

Prices pared some earlier losses in the afternoon trading after a Bloomberg report that China weighed new property stimulus package lifted sentiment.

Coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1), other steelmaking ingredients, extended declines for a third straight session, down 3.17% and 0.7%, respectively, dragged by expectations of sagging demand and rising supply.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange languished. Rebar fell 1.01%, hot-rolled coil shed 0.55%, wire rod (SWRcv1) dipped 0.42% and stainless steel lost 0.45%.

($1 = 7.1145 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Grainger Logs Higher Net Rental Income Despite Challenges
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Grainger posted higher full-year net rental income and EPRA earnings despite marcoeconomic challenges.

The U.K property-investment company said Thursday that net rental income rose to 123.6 million pounds, equivalent to $161.4 million, for the year ended Sept. 30, from 110.1 million pounds the year prior. EPRA earnings--an industry metric--rose to 53.7 million pounds from 48.0 million pounds.

EPRA net asset value was resilient at 298 percentage points, while the overall portfolio valuation rose 0.7%, it added.

The company declared a dividend of 8.31 pence a share, up from 7.55 pence for the prior-year period.

Moving into the new fiscal year, rental growth is expected to be in line with the long-term average of 3% to 3.5%, the company said. EPRA earnings are targeted at 60 million pounds in fiscal 2026, it added.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: CMC Markets Raises Net Operating Income Expectations
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

CMC Markets said its performance for the first half of fiscal 2026 was better than anticipated and raised its net operating income expectations.

The London-listed online trading platform said net operating income for the period ended Sept. 30 rose 5% to 186.2 million pounds ($243.2 million) on higher net trading and investing revenues. This compares with 177.4 million pounds in the same period a year prior.

The group said earnings were boosted by its Australian business, which delivered a record half-year performance from its stockbroking division.

Following its first-half and the strong momentum it carried into the remainder of the year, CMC said it now expects net operating income to be approximately 10% ahead of the 353.9 million pounds analysts expect, according to a company compiled consensus.

Its first-half pretax profit fell 1% to 49.3 million pounds but the company declared an interim dividend of 5.5 pence a share, nearly 80% higher than 3.1 pence last year.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: WETTERI: Q3 revenue up 5%, but non-recurring costs drove an operating loss; debt and equity ratio improved
data.description:
Revenue grew 5% in Q3 2025, but non-recurring restructuring and inventory revaluation costs led to an operating loss. Interest-bearing debt was reduced by €35.5 million, and the equity ratio improved to 19%. The company is focused on profitability and expects full benefits from its program in 2026.

Original document: Wetteri Oyj [WETTERI] Interim report — Nov. 20 2025
